_spawn_isolation_note: Loop body inline in main (Opus); Step-3 reviewer + HALT challenger spawned as independent subagents.
verdict: Good app, but not top-tier yet
verdict_explanation: The three structural anchors are now addressed or in hand: the central record is a named BoardResult, the pure helpers have a real test suite (visible this loop), and this loop extracts the per-board parse into a testable module-level function. What keeps it short of top-tier is the still-large search_boards (transport + filter + date-scan + render remain fused) and two small residuals (a dead wrapper, duplicated render blocks).

architecture_quality.proof: at loop-3 start search_boards still fuses transport+parse+filter+dates+render (soundboard-snag.py:708-...); parse extraction is this loop's fix, scored next loop

state_management.proof: single-threaded; one writer per concern; immutable instance attrs

domain_modeling.proof: BoardResult names the central record; sounds_info still bare 2-tuples and approx_source stringly-typed

data_flow.proof: named-field contract; residual: main re-filters results (soundboard-snag.py:1591) duplicating search_boards' filter (1247)

framework_idioms.proof: idiomatic stdlib; defensive sanitization; HTTPS; no secrets

concurrency.proof: no async/threading — synchronous CLI, no shared-mutable hazard
concurrency.residual_blocking_10: time.sleep pacing is intentional server etiquette, not a determinism hazard
concurrency.residual_rationale_or_backlog_ref: Tool is deliberately synchronous; no concurrency model to make safer. Permanent design carve-out.

simplicity.proof: at loop-3 start the ~734-line function, duplicated render (1190 vs 1367; 1280 vs 1392) and dead wrapper (319) remain; parse extraction is this loop's fix

test_strategy.proof: test_soundboard_snag.py (commit db6f0bf) adds 24 real assertions at the pure-helper Interfaces — sanitization, views/date parsing, slug/header extraction, formatters. Residual: search_boards' parse/network paths still untested (addressed this loop by extracting the pure parse)

credibility.proof: a real test suite now backs the helper claims (commit db6f0bf), and BoardResult makes output self-describing; code remains honest with no fake-clean

strengths: The test suite from loop 2 exercises the genuinely branchy logic (filename sanitization, compact-views parsing) at the real Interfaces — not glue or snapshots
BoardResult continues to make the search engine's output self-describing with no runtime cost
Synchronous design keeps concurrency trivially safe; defensive filename sanitization remains a real strength

findings.title: search_boards is a ~734-line function fusing transport, parsing, filtering, date inference and rendering
findings.why_it_matters: The core scraping logic cannot be tested or reused because it is welded to network I/O and terminal printing.
findings.what_is_wrong: A single function performs HTTP fetching, regex HTML extraction, domain filtering, date inference, sorting and all terminal rendering interleaved inside one deeply nested loop. The HTML extraction is pure and could be a tested Module.
findings.leverage_impact: Maintainers must hold the entire function in their head to change any one concern.
findings.locality_impact: A parsing change risks the rendering and filtering paths because they share one scope and 20+ locals.
findings.why_weakens_submission: Fusing pure parsing with I/O and presentation keeps the testable logic unreachable without the network.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Extract the pure per-board HTML parsing into a module-level _parse_board_html(board_html) -> ParsedBoard, leaving transport/filtering/rendering in search_boards; add fixture-based tests for it. Now de-risked by the helper suite.

findings.title: Dead pass-through wrapper _fetch_last_modified
findings.why_it_matters: Unused indirection adds reading cost and a second name for one behavior with no caller to justify it.
findings.what_is_wrong: _fetch_last_modified wraps _fetch_last_modified_detailed and discards the diagnostic, but nothing in the module calls it.
findings.locality_impact: Minor: a reader must confirm it is unused before trusting the live path.
findings.why_weakens_submission: A pass-through wrapper with no caller is dead weight the deletion test removes cleanly.

findings.title: Date-display and skipped-breakdown rendering duplicated near-verbatim
findings.why_it_matters: Two copies of the same presentation logic must be kept in sync by hand; they will drift.
findings.what_is_wrong: The approx-updated date block and the skipped-buckets breakdown block each appear twice with near-identical bodies.
findings.leverage_impact: A formatting change must be made in two places.
findings.locality_impact: Drift between the two copies produces inconsistent output for the same data.
findings.why_weakens_submission: Duplicate presentation logic is a simplification deduction.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Extract the two blocks into small module-level format helpers taking a BoardResult and the date-stats map.

simplification_check.structurally_necessary: F2 extraction passes the Shallow-module test: the per-board parse is now a Module with a small Interface (html in, ParsedBoard out) and Depth (10 regex extractions behind it), reached directly by tests. No new Seam (in-process pure function), so the Unified Seam Policy does not apply.
simplification_check.helpful_simplification: search_boards shrinks; the parse becomes fixture-testable without network.
simplification_check.should_not_be_done: Do not also extract a parser class hierarchy, a strategy object, or a port — one pure function is the honest shape. Do not extract the date-scan (it is network-bound) this loop.
simplification_check.tests_after_fix: test_soundboard_snag.py gains ParseBoardHtmlTests (full board, play-only, deduped ids, empty html) asserting at the new _parse_board_html Interface. No old tests become shallow (the helper tests stay valid).

backlog.title: Extract pure per-board HTML parsing from search_boards (F2 / F-002)
backlog.why_it_matters: Makes the core scraping logic testable and shrinks the god-function.

backlog.title: De-duplicate the date-display and skipped-breakdown render blocks (F-005)
backlog.why_it_matters: Removes drift-prone copies; further shrinks search_boards.

deepening_candidates.source_friction_proven: Finding F2 — the parse logic was unreachable for testing while fused with I/O
deepening_candidates.why_shallow_or_misplaced: Before this loop there was no parsing Interface at all; the behavior was inlined.
deepening_candidates.behavior_to_move_behind_interface: Regex extraction of board name fields, downloads flag, sound ids/titles, description, category, views, tags from one board's HTML.
deepening_candidates.test_surface_after_change: ParseBoardHtmlTests asserts on synthetic HTML fixtures without network.
deepening_candidates.smallest_first_step: Done this loop: extract _parse_board_html and route search_boards through it.
deepening_candidates.what_not_to_do: Do not add a parser class hierarchy or strategy pattern.

builder_notes.pattern: Pure computation fused inside an I/O loop
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: A block of regex/transform code sits between a network read and a print, sharing the loop's locals; you cannot test it without the network.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Move the pure block to a module-level function taking the raw input and returning a named record; the loop binds locals from it.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: _parse_board_html(board_html) -> ParsedBoard makes 10 regex extractions fixture-testable.

builder_notes.pattern: Return a named record from a parser, not a pile of locals
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: The extracted function would otherwise return a long tuple or set many out-params.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Give the parser a NamedTuple result so call sites and tests read fields by name.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: ParsedBoard(NamedTuple) carries sound_matches, has_downloads, download_ids, ... .

builder_notes.pattern: Test the extracted Interface with a synthetic fixture
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: A newly-extracted pure function has no direct test, only transitive coverage through the I/O caller.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Write a small representative input string and assert each field of the returned record, including an empty-input case.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: a 10-line board HTML string asserts download dedup, tag extraction, views parsing, html-unescape of preview titles.

narrative: Place — now a good app, not yet top-tier. The three structural anchors are handled: named BoardResult, a real helper test suite (visible this loop and lifting test_strategy and credibility), and this loop's extraction of the per-board parse into a fixture-tested _parse_board_html. search_boards still owns transport, filtering, the network date-scan and all rendering, so architecture and simplicity have further to climb (scored next loop). Ownership and concurrency remain trustworthy. Remaining backlog is small and subtractive (dead wrapper, duplicated render); the standing risk is over-reaching toward decomposition the single-file design does not need.

loop_result.what_changed: Extracted the pure per-board HTML parsing out of search_boards into a module-level _parse_board_html(board_html) -> ParsedBoard (NamedTuple); search_boards now calls it and binds locals from the result. Added ParseBoardHtmlTests (4 fixture-based tests) covering a full board, a play-only board, download-id de-duplication, and empty HTML.
loop_result.evidence_change_is_honest: python3 -m py_compile passes; python3 -m unittest test_soundboard_snag runs 28 tests, all OK (24 prior + 4 new parse tests); --help exits 0; the extraction reuses the identical regexes/dedup/unescape logic so it is behavior-preserving; grep confirms the parse regexes (item-desc, btn-download-track, Category/Views/Tags) no longer appear in the search_boards body (only the render-label print remains), and search_boards shrank from ~734 to ~694 lines.

implementation_review.reason: F-002 is genuinely fixed: _parse_board_html and ParsedBoard exist, all parse regexes are gone from search_boards, all locals are correctly rebound at the call site, the counter/status/preview_count computations survive, and the four ParseBoardHtmlTests assert real field values at the new Interface.
